name: Scandinavian Defense
description: An immediate challenge to e4 with d5. Direct and easy to understand.

moves.commentary: The Scandinavian - an immediate strike at White's center.

children.commentary: exd5 captures the pawn. Black must decide how to recapture.
Qxd5 recaptures. The queen is active but may become a target.
Nc3 develops with tempo, attacking the queen.
Qa5 keeps the queen active and defends the c7 pawn.
d4 stakes a claim in the center.
Nf3 develops and prepares Bf4 or Bg5.

tree.commentary: Qxd5 recaptures. The queen is active.

strategy.keyIdea: Immediately challenge White's e4 pawn with ...d5, accepting a slight development deficit in exchange for early piece activity.
strategy.middleGamePlan: After recapturing the pawn with the queen (usually to d6 or d5), develop quickly and look to use the open d-file. The queen on d6 or d5 can be a target, so piece activity must compensate. Queenside development with ...Bg4, ...Nc6, and then castling queenside or kingside depending on the position is the typical plan.
strategy.watchOut: Leaving the queen exposed in the center — after ...Qxd5, White gains time with Nc3 attacking it, so you must develop fast enough to keep up.
typicalGoals: Develop pieces quickly to justify the early queen sortie
Use the semi-open d-file for rook pressure
Activate the dark-squared bishop to g4 or f5
Castle quickly before White can exploit the development lead

quiz.question: You played the Scandinavian and recaptured with ...Qxd5. White plays Nc3 attacking your queen. What's the priority?

options.text: Retreat the queen to d8 to safety
options.explanation: Retreating to d8 loses a full tempo and undoes the reason for playing ...d5 in the first place. Move the queen to an active square instead.

options.text: Move the queen to d6 or a5 and develop another piece
options.explanation: Correct. Moving the queen to an active square (d6 or a5) and immediately developing another piece keeps the position dynamic and compensates for the tempo lost.

options.text: Play ...c6 to support the queen on d5
options.explanation: ...c6 is sometimes played but blocks natural piece development to c6. The Scandinavian works best with quick piece activity, not pawn consolidation.

default.opponentMoveSummary: Your opponent stepped off the main theory line with an unusual move, choosing their own path rather than the expected response.
default.whyBookBreaks: The standard line assumes a particular pawn or piece configuration that this move bypasses.
default.opponentStrategy: Your opponent is trying to reach a less-charted position where their preparation or intuition gives them an edge.
forwardGuidance: Prioritize completing your development — get all minor pieces off the back rank.
Control the center with pawns or piece pressure before launching any flank operations.

Nf3.opponentMoveSummary: White played Nf3, developing the knight naturally and delaying Nc3.
Nf3.whyBookBreaks: After 2.exd5 Qxd5 3.Nc3, the book expects Nc3 to attack the queen. Nf3 develops naturally and keeps options open.
Nf3.opponentStrategy: White delays Nc3 — develops naturally, may transpose to main lines, or keeps flexibility for a different setup.
forwardGuidance: Complete development; White's Nf3 often transposes to known lines.
Be ready for Nc3 next; the queen may need to relocate.

d4.opponentMoveSummary: White played d4, staking an immediate claim in the center before Black completes development.
d4.whyBookBreaks: The book expects Nc3 after the queen capture. d4 claims the center immediately and changes the character of the position.
d4.opponentStrategy: White plays d4 immediately — stakes central control and aims to restrict Black's development.
forwardGuidance: Complete development; White's center can be challenged with ...e5 or ...c6.
Consider ...Nc6 or ...Nf6 to develop and challenge the center.

c4.opponentMoveSummary: White played c4, gaining queenside space and challenging Black's queen (Portuguese Variation setup).
c4.whyBookBreaks: The book expects Nc3. c4 gains queenside space and attacks the queen on d5, forcing it to move.
c4.opponentStrategy: Portuguese Variation setup — White gains queenside space and challenges Black's queen, aiming for a different structure.
forwardGuidance: Relocate the queen to a safe square (a5 or d8); c4 attacks it.
Complete development; the Portuguese can lead to sharp, tactical play.
